authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS/OCR is the federal HIPAA Privacy Rule enforcement authority, and this official guidance directly governs PHI use, disclosure, and minimum-necessary handling for patient, member, and caregiver communications.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy-sensitive intake, identity and consent checks, communication drafting, and PHI disclosure escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Managed Care Manual, Chapter 13: Medicare Managed Care Beneficiary Grievances, Organization Determinations, and Appeals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the federal Medicare program administrator, and this manual is authoritative public payer operating guidance for grievances, organization determinations, appeals, and coverage-sensitive routing boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, authorization, referral, coverage-dependency, grievance, denial, and appeal handoff handling

authoritative_sources.title: HCAHPS: Patients' Perspectives of Care Survey
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers HCAHPS as a national public patient-experience measure, making it authoritative for communication, responsiveness, discharge-information, and transition-related experience terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: experience classification, communication quality, discharge or care-transition communication, and complaint-resolution evidence framing

authoritative_sources.title: CAHPS Surveys and Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is the federal steward for CAHPS surveys and guidance on patient experience with health plans, providers, and systems, which makes it authoritative for experience and communication concepts without creating clinical decision authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: experience taxonomy, stakeholder communication, patient or member satisfaction evidence, and complaint pattern escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality, travel, accessibility, consumer-protection, or payment-security guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal ADA Title III enforcement authority, and its lodging reservation provisions are directly authoritative for accessible-room descriptions, reservation parity, and third-party reservation-service duties.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible lodging request intake, guest-room feature communication, and accessibility-sensitive handoff

authoritative_sources.title: Passengers with Disabilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality, travel, accessibility, consumer-protection, or payment-security guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOT enforces air-travel consumer protection and Air Carrier Access Act requirements, making this official guidance authoritative for disability-assistance and passenger-rights boundaries in travel guest-experience cases.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: passenger assistance intake, accessible travel support, airline-owner routing, and travel disruption communication

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality, travel, accessibility, consumer-protection, or payment-security guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official guidance covers fee disclosure for short-term lodging and live-event ticketing, which constrains guest-facing price, fee, and refund communication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fee complaint intake, folio explanation, refund or compensation escalation, and conflicting policy handling

authoritative_sources.title: Are call center environments considered sensitive areas for PCI DSS Requirement 9.1.1?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality, travel, accessibility, consumer-protection, or payment-security guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and this FAQ directly constrains payment-card handling in guest-support contact-center workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-sensitive guest support, folio dispute communication, contact-center handling, and payment-data refusal

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify the requester, channel, guest or patient/member relationship, property or itinerary, service dates, payer or plan context, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Public hospitality, travel, accessibility, consumer-protection, or payment-security guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite, privacy, and record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate identity, consent, minimum-necessary handling, eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, encounter or care-transition documentation, claim or denial records, reservation, itinerary, folio, and service-recovery evidence before responding.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Guest-experience evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Review PMS, CRS, CRM, contact-center, case-management, EHR/EMR, practice-management, payer-portal, utilization-review, claim, incident, and service-recovery systems to establish the truth set and distinguish documented facts from assumptions.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public hospitality, travel, accessibility, consumer-protection, or payment-security guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported resolution or stakeholder communication
workflow_stages.description: Prepare a record-backed disposition or communication summary that explains what is known, what action was taken, what remains unresolved, and which owner must act next without overstating authority.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public hospitality, travel, accessibility, consumer-protection, or payment-security guidance

workflow_stages.name: Policy exception, repeat-failure, and unsupported-workaround escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route refund, compensation, eligibility, authorization, service-recovery, accessibility, coverage, safety, clinical, privacy, payer, or conflicting-policy exceptions into an escalation packet with the required facts and owner.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Public hospitality, travel, accessibility, consumer-protection, or payment-security guidance

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and audit trail
workflow_stages.description: Confirm resolution only when the authoritative record set supports closure, communication logging is complete, unresolved items are assigned, and any PHI or guest-sensitive handling constraints are preserved.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: confirmation numbers, PNRs, itineraries, folios, room or transfer records, stay dates, supplier references, and guest profile records
service tickets, CRM notes, contact-center transcripts, guest messaging threads, complaint logs, disruption notices, incident notes, and service-recovery histories
eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, patient or member status, payer, plan, provider type, facility type, service setting, and service-date records
encounter documentation, orders, clinical-administrative notes, discharge or care-transition records, case-management notes, and utilization-review notes
charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, payment, refund, voucher, and compensation approval records
privacy, consent, identity-proofing, representative authorization, minimum-necessary, and approved communication-channel evidence
accessibility notes, accessible-room features, passenger assistance requests, disability accommodation records, and approved supplier or property handoff notes
systems_of_record: travel itinerary or supplier management system

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, informational, complaint-driven, exception-driven, owner-transfer sensitive, or low confidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, consent/privacy context, eligibility, referral, authorization, payer, plan, and service context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed service dispositions, resolution notes, communication summaries, and next-step packages without overstating authority.
Apply documented guest-experience, service-recovery, accessibility, privacy, and communication rules when tenant policy and authoritative records explicitly support them.
Package escalations and handoffs when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required reservation, itinerary, folio, guest, patient/member, eligibility, referral, authorization, encounter, claim, denial, appeal, privacy, consent, payment, incident, or service-recovery records are missing or materially conflict.

The request depends on clinical judgment, diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, licensed review, payer-policy interpretation, utilization-management approval, coding, claim adjustment, appeal merit, privacy interpretation, or legal interpretation.
A policy exception, refund, compensation, pricing, eligibility, accessibility, safety, fraud, payment-card, or repeated service failure exceeds delegated authority.
Public guidance conflicts with tenant, payer, plan, provider, property, supplier, brand, processor, or jurisdiction policy.
refuse: Promise a refund, fee waiver, compensation, accessible-room substitution, travel exception, authorization approval, coverage result, claim adjustment, or clinical outcome unsupported by authoritative records and delegated policy.
Disclose or request PHI or sensitive guest/payment information without the required identity, consent, authorization, channel, and minimum-necessary controls.
Interpret diagnosis, treatment plan, medical necessity, payer policy, coding, denial, appeal, or clinical-risk facts.
Bypass eligibility, referral, authorization, documentation, privacy, safety, fraud, or payment-security controls to satisfy the stakeholder.

domain_failure_modes: the lane treats a patient-traveler or member support request as generic hospitality service and misses eligibility, referral, authorization, PHI, or payer dependencies
a guest-experience note discloses diagnosis, appointment, claim, or caregiver information without minimum-necessary controls
a payer-funded lodging or transport request is resolved without confirming eligibility, benefits, service dates, referral, authorization, or plan context
the lane implies clinical advice, discharge safety, medical necessity, coverage approval, denial reversal, or appeal merit
a refund, compensation, fee waiver, voucher, accessible-room substitution, or travel exception is promised outside documented policy and approval authority
repeat failures are handled as ordinary apology cases rather than escalation-worthy complaint, grievance, or service-recovery patterns
conflicting public, payer, provider, property, supplier, or tenant policies are synthesized into a confident answer instead of escalated
the package collapses into a generic customer-service script and omits hospitality/travel artifacts, healthcare-administrative records, and traceability

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Hospitality-and-travel label with healthcare-and-care-delivery domain profile
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: The package treats hospitality/travel guest experience as the lane context and applies healthcare profile guardrails whenever patient, member, payer, referral, authorization, PHI, clinical-risk, or claim-administrative facts govern the request.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific lodging, travel, Medicaid, payer, and privacy variation
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution: No single public baseline is substituted for tenant, payer, provider, property, supplier, plan, state Medicaid, or jurisdiction rules; cases that turn on those differences require retrieval and escalation if precedence is unresolved.

research_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack use.

The research output constrains scenarios to prove guest-experience resolution, healthcare-administrative prerequisite handling, PHI controls, clinical/payer boundaries, policy exceptions, handoffs, low-confidence escalation, and tenant adaptation.
